tagline: Every team has a twin. History already played this game.

round_of_64.reasoning: No 16-seed has beaten a 1-seed more than once in tournament history. Houston's defensive profile mirrors 2003 Syracuse.
9-seeds have won 51% of 8/9 matchups historically. Texas A&M's physical style resembles 2016 Butler's grit.
12-over-5 upsets occur 35% of the time. James Madison's unbeaten conference run echoes 2013 FGCU's fearlessness.
Duke's talent floor is too high for a 13-seed upset here. Resembles 2019 Duke's overwhelming athleticism.
11-seeds with late-season momentum have a strong upset history. NC State's ACC tournament run mirrors 2011 VCU's energy.
3-seeds beat 14-seeds about 85% of the time. Kentucky's brand and talent historically dominate early rounds.
Florida's tournament pedigree gives them an edge in close matchups. Similar to 2017 Florida's balanced attack.
2-seeds lose to 15-seeds less than 6% of the time. Marquette's depth mirrors 2013 Miami's efficiency.
UConn's historical dominance as a 1-seed is unmatched. Their 2023 championship run was a generational performance.
True coin flip, but Northwestern's recent tournament experience gives them a slight archetype edge over FAU's post-Cinderella hangover.
San Diego State's defensive identity matches 2023 SDSU's run. They grind out these games.
Auburn's athleticism overwhelms Ivy League teams historically. Similar mismatch to 2019 Auburn's early rounds.
6-seeds hold a historical edge over 11-seeds at about 63%. BYU's scoring depth fits the 2011 BYU mold.
Illinois' size and Big Ten toughness match 2005 Illinois' floor-spacing attack. 14-seeds rarely have the firepower.
Drake's efficient half-court offense mirrors 2018 Loyola Chicago's patient, upset-ready style.
Iowa State's defensive rating is elite. Historically, 2-seeds handle 15-seeds with defensive identities.
Purdue's Zach Edey is a generational big man. Historical comps to Shaq-era LSU suggest total dominance.
TCU's Big 12 experience and tournament toughness give them the archetype edge in a true toss-up.
Gonzaga's March pedigree is historically elite. They've been a 5-or-better seed seven straight years.
Kansas' blue-blood tournament DNA dominates 4/13 matchups. Similar to 2022 Kansas' championship pedigree.
Oregon's Pac-12 tournament run and 11-seed archetype match 2016 Oregon's Sweet 16 run.
Creighton's offensive firepower is historically too much for a 14-seed. Matches 2021 Creighton's shooting profile.
Texas' talent and size historically prevail in 7/10 matchups. Echoes 2021 Texas' physicality.
Saint Peter's pulled the miracle in 2022, but lightning rarely strikes twice. Tennessee's defense is suffocating.
North Carolina's 1-seed pedigree is historically near-automatic. Matches 2017 UNC's championship DNA.
Tom Izzo is historically the greatest 8/9 game coach. Michigan State's March pedigree is unmatched.
Saint Mary's patient offensive style mirrors 2010 Saint Mary's tournament success. Methodical wins.
Alabama's three-point volume overwhelms mid-majors. Similar to 2023 Alabama's explosive offense.
Clemson's ACC pedigree and defensive identity fit the 6-seed-advances archetype.
Baylor's championship experience and size mismatch mirror 2021 Baylor's dominant early rounds.
Dayton's crowd energy and mid-major tournament pedigree match 2014 Dayton's upset magic.
Arizona's offensive efficiency is historically elite for a 2-seed. Long Beach State lacks the firepower.

round_of_32.reasoning: Houston's defensive archetype historically smothers mid-seeds. Matches 2011 VCU's opponent—except Houston is the juggernaut.
Duke's talent advantage reasserts in the second weekend. 12-seeds winning twice in a row is historically rare.
Kentucky's talent historically prevails against hot mid-seeds. Matches 2014 Kentucky's elite athleticism.
2-seeds advance past 7-seeds about 67% of the time. Marquette's depth mirrors 2003 Marquette's balance.
UConn's 2023 championship DNA and returning core make them historically one of the strongest repeat threats.
San Diego State's defensive archetype historically frustrates athletic teams. Mirrors 2023 SDSU's tournament grind.
Illinois' size and defensive versatility match 2005 Illinois' deep run profile. 3-seeds advance 70% here.
Iowa State's pressure defense historically dismantles mid-major offenses. Matches 2000 Iowa State's turnover-forcing identity.
Purdue's size advantage is historically unprecedented. Edey's dominance mirrors Hakeem's college impact.
Kansas' blue-blood pedigree in 4/5 matchups historically gives them the edge. Matches 2018 Kansas' grit.
Creighton's three-point shooting historically overwhelms mid-seed defenses. 3-seeds win this matchup 68% of the time.
Tennessee's defensive identity historically carries them deep. Matches 2019 Tennessee's suffocating style.
North Carolina's scoring depth overwhelms Izzo's grind-it-out style. Mirrors 2017 UNC's offensive firepower.
Alabama's tempo and athleticism historically overpower patient mid-major offenses. Matches 2023 Alabama's run.
Baylor's size and shooting balance mirror 2021 Baylor's championship run. 3-seeds have the edge here.
Arizona's offensive efficiency historically dominates 7-seeds. Matches 2014 Arizona's tournament ceiling.

sweet_16.reasoning: Houston's defense historically strangles blue-blood offenses. Mirrors 2021 Houston's Final Four path.
Marquette's balanced attack matches 2003 Marquette's Sweet 16 upset profile. Kentucky's inconsistency is a historical weakness.
UConn's offensive ceiling historically overwhelms defensive-first teams. Rematch of 2023 title game favors UConn.
Illinois' size and scoring balance mirror 2005 Illinois' Final Four run. Big Ten teams historically excel in this round.
Purdue's dominant interior game historically overwhelms even blue-blood defenses. Edey is matchup-proof.
Tennessee's defense historically suffocates perimeter-dependent teams. Matches 2019 Tennessee's grit.
North Carolina's championship pedigree historically shines in Sweet 16 matchups. Mirrors 2022 UNC's deep run.
Arizona's tempo and three-point shooting mirror 2014 Arizona's Sweet 16 dominance. Baylor lacks a historical comp this deep.

elite_8.reasoning: Houston's Elite 8 profile mirrors 2021 Houston's Final Four run. Their defense historically peaks in high-pressure games.
UConn's back-to-back title pursuit mirrors 2007 Florida's repeat. Historical repeaters are rare but dominant.
Purdue's size advantage is historically decisive in Elite 8 games. Tennessee lacks a counter for Edey.
North Carolina's tournament DNA in Elite 8 games is historically elite. Mirrors 2017 UNC's championship resolve.

final_four.reasoning: UConn's repeat bid mirrors 2007 Florida. Historical back-to-back champions are unstoppable in the Final Four.
Purdue's dominant big man archetype historically prevails when the stage is biggest. Matches 2018 Villanova's patient efficiency.

championship.reasoning: Purdue's interior dominance mirrors Virginia 2019's patient title run. History says the team with the size advantage wins close championship games.
